error.signature: SendGrid batch send returns 202 Accepted but some recipients don't receive email

verdict.summary: SendGrid's v3 mail/send API returns 202 Accepted (queued) not 200 (sent). A 202 means SendGrid accepted the request, not that delivery succeeded. Batch sends with invalid/bounced recipients silently skip those recipients. No per-recipient status in the API response.

dead_ends.action: Assume 202 response means all recipients will receive the email
dead_ends.why_fails: 202 = queued for sending, not delivered. Invalid addresses, bounced domains, and spam-flagged recipients are silently dropped after queuing.

dead_ends.action: Send to large batches without checking bounce/suppression lists
dead_ends.why_fails: SendGrid maintains a suppression list. Addresses on it are silently skipped. Sending to previously-bounced addresses hurts your sender reputation.

workarounds.action: Set up Event Webhooks to track per-recipient delivery status
workarounds.how: SendGrid Settings -> Event Webhooks -> Enable delivered, bounced, dropped events. Track delivery per recipient.

workarounds.action: Check suppression list before sending batches
Use personalizations array for per-recipient tracking
workarounds.how: Each personalization gets a unique message_id. Use this to track delivery status per recipient group.
